tickets.title: US004: Advisor prerequisite override
tickets.description:
As an advisor, I want to override prerequisite requirements for approved students so that I can allow special enrollment exceptions.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The advisor can approve a prerequisite override for a student.
2. The system records the advisor's name and the override reason.
3. The student can enroll in the course after the override is approved.

tickets.title: US005: Full class waitlist planning
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to add full courses to my academic roadmap so that I can plan future semesters even when classes are currently full.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The student can add a full course to the roadmap as a planned course.
2. The system clearly marks the course as currently full.
3. The roadmap retains the planned course after saving.

tickets.title: US001: Course prerequisite checking
tickets.description:
As a student, I want the system to automatically check course prerequisites so that I can know whether I am eligible to enroll in a course.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system displays a warning when prerequisites are not completed.
2. The system allows a course to be added when all prerequisites are completed.
3. The system displays all prerequisite courses in the course details.

tickets.title: US002: Academic roadmap planning
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to create a multi-semester academic roadmap so that I can plan my path to graduation.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The student can add courses to future semesters.
2. The roadmap displays all planned courses by semester.
3. The system saves the roadmap after the student confirms changes.

tickets.title: US003: Schedule conflict detection
tickets.description:
As a student, I want the system to detect schedule conflicts so that I can avoid enrolling in overlapping classes.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system warns the student when two selected courses have overlapping schedules.
2. The system identifies the conflicting courses in the warning message.
3. The student can review conflicts before finalizing the roadmap.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 1: Security: Students must not be able to access another student's schedu
tickets.description: Security: Students must not be able to access another student's schedule or personal academic information.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 2: Auditability: The system must record all advisor prerequisite override
tickets.description: Auditability: The system must record all advisor prerequisite overrides for auditing purposes.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 3: Usability: The system should provide an intuitive drag-and-drop interf
tickets.description: Usability: The system should provide an intuitive drag-and-drop interface for academic planning.

analysis.ambiguity1: The requirement "The course catalog is ugly" is too vague because it does not specify what aspects of the catalog need improvement. There are no measurable usability, design, or functionality criteria that developers can implement.
analysis.ambiguity2: The requirement to warn students about 8:00 AM classes is vague because it is unclear whether the warning should apply to all students or only to those who choose to receive such notifications. The requirement does not define the warning rules.
analysis.conflictSolution: There is a conflict between the Registrar, who wants to prevent students from adding full classes to their roadmap, and the Head of Advising, who wants students to plan four years ahead. The solution is to allow future courses to be added as planned courses when availability is unknown, while current-term courses must check real-time seat availability.
analysis.nfr1: Security: Students must not be able to access another student's schedule or personal academic information.
analysis.nfr2: Auditability: The system must record all advisor prerequisite overrides for auditing purposes.
analysis.nfr3: Usability: The system should provide an intuitive drag-and-drop interface for academic planning.
